zotero.abstractNote: Simulation-based inference (SBI) allows for Bayesian parameter inference in models where the likelihood is intractable but samples can be generated. A common challenge in SBI is that the data often contains many uninformative features, which can hinder the performance of inference algorithms. Here, we propose a method for identifying informative features in SBI. Our approach is based on learning a summary statistic that is both informative about the parameters and low-dimensional. We show that our method can be used to perform efficient inference in high-dimensional settings and that it outperforms existing methods on a range of benchmarks.
zotero.conferenceName: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems

extra.Editors: S. Koyejo and S. Mohamed and A. Agarwal and D. Belgrave and K. Cho and A. Oh

zotero.proceedingsTitle: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 35 (NeurIPS 2022)
